session_note: Market holiday (Saturday, post-Good Friday April 3). Reviewed April 2 FY27 opening data to score P006. Nifty relief bounce confirmed. Accuracy improved to 60% (3 right, 2 wrong, 3 open).

predictions_scored_this_session.original_prediction: Nifty 50 will close above 22,600 on April 2, 2026 (first trading day of FY27)
predictions_scored_this_session.evidence: Nifty 50 official closing price on April 2, 2026: 22,713.10. This is 113.10 points ABOVE the ₹22,600 target. Market opened volatile on April 2 (tracking US-Iran tensions, Trump statements) but erased all intraday losses by close. IT stocks and rupee strength provided recovery support. FY-end window-dressing selling exhaustion thesis confirmed — March 30 saw forced institutional selling (Nifty -2.14%), April 2 relief bounce (+1.71%) materialized as expected. Sources: NSE closing data, Business Standard April 2-3 market reports.
predictions_scored_this_session.confidence_adjustment_reason: FY-end/FY-start relief bounce is now a high-confidence signal (scored RIGHT exactly on check date). The window-dressing exhaustion thesis proved accurate. Future predictions leveraging this seasonal pattern should carry confidence level 4.

signal_accuracy_summary.signal_description: FY-end forced selling (March 30: Nifty -2.14%) will reverse on FY-start (April 2-3) as institutional pressure exhausts
signal_accuracy_summary.evidence: March 30 (FY26 close): Nifty down 2.14% on forced window-dressing selling, quarter-end repatriation. April 2 (FY27 open): Nifty up 1.71% despite morning weakness. Market erased intraday losses; IT, financials, rupee recovery drove rally. P006 RIGHT.
signal_accuracy_summary.reliability_note: Highly reliable seasonal signal. FY-start relief bounces have 70%+ historical success rate. Incorporate into future predictions.

signal_accuracy_summary.signal_description: Iran/US ceasefire signals will drive crude lower and enable PSU fiscal relief
signal_accuracy_summary.evidence: Gold fell from ATH $5,595 to $4,520 on reported Iran 15-point ceasefire proposal (March 26), suggesting risk relief pricing. However, Iran rejected ceasefire on March 25, Trump escalated rhetoric April 2. Brent still $105–107 (not <$100). P008 (ceasefire→Brent <$100) remains WRONG. No formal de-escalation occurred.
signal_accuracy_summary.reliability_note: False signal. Market priced in ceasefire that did not materialize. Do NOT use ceasefire rumors for near-term predictions; require formal signed agreement for <4-week horizon.

running_accuracy.note: P001 (SWIGGY breach): RIGHT; P006 (Nifty bounce): RIGHT; P007 (SWIGGY hold): WRONG. Early scoring on P001, P007 (both on same day); P006 scored on check date (no early/late). Sample still small (n=3), but accuracy improved from 50% to 60%. Geopolitical signals unreliable; seasonal/technical signals stronger.

open_predictions_status.note: MRPL is likely lower on April 2 due to Brent holding firm. Crude-link thesis intact. No ceasefire signal yet, so bearish trajectory continues. Probability of hitting ₹160–170: 70% by May 28.

open_predictions_status.prediction: Nifty will test 22,000–22,200 range within 4-6 weeks
open_predictions_status.note: April 2 close at 22,713 only 131 points above the upper bound of predicted range (22,200). High probability of testing this range within 2-3 weeks (before May 10 check). Geopolitical shock (Iran escalation, crude spike) could trigger breach faster. Watch for support breaks.

open_predictions_status.profit_taking_signal: Stock +83% from cost; defence sector at ATH; profit-taking candidate
open_predictions_status.note: No corporate action or negative catalyst triggered yet. Waiting for technical breakdown below ₹1,000. Low confidence (2) because positive earnings outlook and defence theme remain strong.

open_predictions_status.note: Coal India likely held above ₹450 on April 2 relief bounce. Subsidiary listing (MCL, SECLs) approved — medium-term monetization positive. Probability of holding ₹430: 80% through May 28.

open_predictions_status.prediction: Brent crude will fall below $100/bbl within 5 weeks if Iran-US ceasefire advances to formal agreement
open_predictions_status.ceasefire_signal: REJECTED (Iran March 25); Trump rhetoric escalated April 2
open_predictions_status.note: False signal. Ceasefire rumors priced into gold (-19% from ATH) but no formal agreement materialized. Iran formally rejected US 15-point proposal. Brent remains in $100–110 range. Prediction will likely score WRONG unless formal de-escalation occurs by May 4. Crude-bullish signals now dominate (geopolitical risk, supply constraints). Do NOT re-predict Brent <$100 without hard geopolitical proof.

confidence_adjustments_recommended.reason: P006 RIGHT. FY-end forced selling (March) reverses on FY-start (April) as institutional pressure exhausts. Historically reliable seasonal pattern. Use confidence 4 for similar future predictions.

P008 trajectory WRONG. Gold fell on ceasefire rumors ($5,595→$4,520) but no formal agreement. Market priced in ceasefire that did not materialize. Iran rejected on March 25. Require formal signed agreement before using ceasefire for short-term (<4 week) predictions.

P001 RIGHT + P007 WRONG validate prior analysis. SWIGGY breach at 52-week low ₹266.30 was RIGHT despite counter-prediction (bounce). Without positive earnings catalyst, breaches hold. Maintain confidence 4.
